calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Scott Hodges (Administrator).

The Court questions the request to distribute to the two Nieces when the pour-over will says it is supposed to go to the Trustee. Mr. Conrad stated that the Successor Trustee (David Neil) is not willing to serve and wants nothing to do with it, made reference to Probate Code 17200 which allows the Court to determine beneficiaries, stated that the Trust is silent and there is no extrinsic evidence, that Mr. Neil signed a deed to Mr. Hodges, that the transaction was insured and that the other two owners assumed the loan. Mr. Conrad also stated that the Decedent's share of the property was one third, that there were settlement negotiations, that the property sold for more than the equity amount, that the Trust has no provision for appointment of a Successor Trustee, that both of the named Trustees signed declinations to serve and that the Nieces are the ones entitled to distribution. The First and Final Account and Report, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Continued To Jun-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Continued To Jun-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Title Of Real Property And For Imposition Of Reslting Trust
Off Calendar Jun-04-2015 Off Calendar

Petition For Determination Of Title Of Real Property And For Imposition Of Reslting Trust
Off Calendar May-13-2015 Continued To Jul-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad & Melanie O'Brien for (Administrator) Scott Hodges.

The Court stated that it has concerns regarding the approach, that the trust is not invalid, that it moves property, names beneficiaries, that it does not have directions on what to do at death, that it does have problems which are different than being invalid and asked why did they not have the trustee ask for instructions on how to deal with the property. Mr. Conrad stated that they had not considered that point, that no power was given to the trustee, that there is a big hole in the notes and that the trustee had not bee aware of the trust or have knowledge of who the beneficiaries were to be. Mr. Conrad further stated that the trustee does not want to be involved and that this is the most efficient way to get to who the beneficiaries should be. The Court stated that it is not going to proceed with the premise that this is not a valid trust and will continue the matter for the party to decide how to proceed. The Petition for Determination of Title of Real Property and for Imposition of Resulting Trust was continued to July 8, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Lopes & Santiago. (Not Reported)
